dimension_explanations.alignment: The alignment score of 35/50 is high, primarily driven by the extreme urgency of the poverty and displacement crisis in Syria [1]. The organization maintains a medium fit for Muslim donors by offering zakat-eligible programs and seasonal assistance projects that align with Islamic charitable pillars [3].
dimension_explanations.impact: The impact score of 14/50 is considered low because there is currently no third-party verification of program effectiveness or specific data on the cost per beneficiary [4]. While the foundation provides direct services such as orphan sponsorship, the lack of available financial metrics like program expense ratios limits the ability to assess its operational efficiency [2].

baseline_narrative.headline: Yateem Foundation USA provides essential food and shelter to displaced Syrian orphans with the goal of returning them to their original cities.
strengths: Direct service model providing food and shelter to vulnerable populations
Clear mission focus on the Syrian displacement crisis
baseline_narrative.summary: Yateem Foundation USA is a non-profit organization dedicated to supporting displaced children in Syria through comprehensive orphan sponsorship and seasonal assistance projects [1][2]. The organization is recognized as zakat-eligible, allowing donors to fulfill their religious obligations through various giving methods [3].

strengths.detail: The foundation utilizes a <cite id="1">direct service model</cite> to provide <cite id="1">food, shelter, and village construction</cite> for orphans.
The organization operates in the <cite id="3">extreme poverty cause area</cite>, serving <cite id="3">underserved populations</cite> in Syria and Türkiye.
Verified <cite id="4">zakat-eligible status</cite> allows donors to support <cite id="4">fuqara and masakin</cite> through religious giving.

strengths_deep_dive: Yateem Foundation USA distinguishes itself through its <cite id="1">village construction projects</cite> which provide long-term stability for displaced children. By focusing on <cite id="1">returning orphans to their original cities</cite>, the charity moves beyond temporary relief toward <cite id="1">reintegration and permanent housing</cite>. Their <cite id="2">orphanage in Türkiye</cite> serves as a critical cross-border hub for care, while <cite id="4">seasonal projects like Ramadan baskets</cite> ensure immediate nutritional needs are met during peak times of religious significance for the community.
rich_narrative.summary: Yateem Foundation USA is a humanitarian organization dedicated to providing <cite id="1">essential food and shelter to displaced Syrian orphans</cite> with the ultimate objective of <cite id="1">returning them to their original cities</cite>. Operating primarily in <cite id="1">Syria and Türkiye</cite>, the organization manages a portfolio of direct service programs including <cite id="1">orphan sponsorship, seasonal assistance projects, and Ramadan food baskets</cite>. A key pillar of their infrastructure is the <cite id="1">construction of villages for Syrian orphans</cite> and the maintenance of an <cite id="2">orphanage in Türkiye</cite>. As an emerging entity, the foundation focuses on the <cite id="3">extreme poverty cause area</cite>, addressing the immediate needs of <cite id="3">vulnerable populations in conflict zones</cite>. The organization is <cite id="4">recognized as zakat-eligible</cite>, specifically serving the <cite id="4">fuqara (poor) and masakin (needy)</cite> categories of asnaf. While the mission is highly targeted, the organization currently operates with <cite id="5">limited public financial data</cite>, as it is not yet <cite id="5">fully rated by Charity Navigator</cite>. Despite these data gaps, the foundation maintains a <cite id="6">direct service delivery model</cite>, ensuring that aid reaches beneficiaries through <cite id="6">tangible interventions like village construction</cite> and direct sponsorship.
